heading: Training programs for maximum vocational potential for blind
label: 107d–4 Training programs for maximum vocational potential for blind
text: The Commissioner shall insure, through promulgation of appropriate regulations, that uniform and effective training programs, including on-the-job training, are provided for blind individuals, through services under the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 [ et seq.]. He shall further insure that State agencies provide programs for upward mobility (including further education and additional training or retraining for improved work opportunities) for all trainees under this chapter, and that follow-along services are provided to such trainees to assure that their maximum vocational potential is achieved.29 U.S.C. 701
notes: References in TextThe Rehabilitation Act of 1973, referred to in text, is , , , which is classified generally to chapter 16 (§ 701 et seq.) of Title 29, Labor.
